content.text: Dual-wielding skirmisher. Abilities provide active tools and techniques around the passive Combo/Momentum augment system — movement options, attack modifiers, and defensive tools for a class that lives or dies on tempo.

Fluid Movement (Passive): After making a melee attack, move 5ft (1 tile) for free (0 AP, doesn't provoke opportunity attacks). This triggers after EVERY melee attack — two attacks means two free 5ft movements. The Dancer is never standing still. This is the foundation of the entire class — free repositioning between every swing. Combined with Zephyr's Dance of Blades, each hit generates 10ft of free movement.
T2 — Trained Techniques (300 cr each, 1 downtime)

rows: When an enemy misses you in melee, make a free counterattack with your off-hand (0 AP, off-hand accuracy applies). Once per round. Enemy misses feed your Combo Counter — the miss becomes hit #1 of your next turn's chain.
When dual wielding, if both attacks hit the same target this turn, the second deals +2 flat bonus damage. Consistent paired hits are rewarded.
Move up to your full movement. No opportunity attacks. Clean exit. The escape tool — when you're pinned and need to reset position without getting punished.
Your next melee attack this turn ignores the target's Evasion bonus from shields and cover. You fake them out of position. Setup for a guaranteed hit to start the combo.
Once per round: when hit by a ranged attack, reduce the damage by your Reflex bonus. You twist out of the shot's path. Light armor means ranged hits hurt — this mitigates the worst of it.
Switching weapons costs 0 AP (normally 1 AP). Relevant when you want to swap between weapon types mid-combat, or retrieve a thrown weapon.

content.text: T3 — Advanced Techniques (600 cr each, 2 downtime)

rows: Each consecutive hit without a miss grants +1 accuracy to the next attack. Stacks with Combo Counter bonuses. A 4-hit combo with Momentum: hits 1 → 2 → 3 → 4, gaining +1 → +2 → +3 cumulative accuracy on top of Combo bonuses. Devastating precision through rhythm. Resets on miss.
Your next 2 attacks this turn cost 0 AP. You move so fast the AP system can't keep up. Once per encounter. Enables a turn with 4+ attacks. The combo-starting burst.
If you moved this turn, all your melee attacks get +1 accuracy. Always active for a moving Dancer — which is every turn. Permanent +1 accuracy for doing what you already do.

When an enemy attacks you in melee and misses, redirect their attack to an adjacent enemy. Use the original attack roll against the new target's Evasion. Once per round. Their swing hits their ally. You weren't there.

When dual wielding, if your first attack applies a condition (Bleed, Poison, Burn from weapon mods), your second attack on the same target applies it again automatically on hit. Double condition stacking — a poisoned blade hits twice, poisons twice.

Until your next turn: the next attack against you has disadvantage (roll twice, take worse). Your recent movement leaves a ghost of where you were. Once per encounter. Emergency defense for the turn you know you're getting focused.

content.text: T4 — Master Techniques (1,000 cr each, 3 downtime)

rows: Make 3 melee attacks against a single target (alternating main/off-hand). Each attack is resolved independently. All 3 count for Combo Counter. Once per encounter. The combo-starting nuke — 3 hits in one action sets up Combo 3 (Triple) immediately.

At Combo 4+: you may choose to RESET the Combo Counter to 0 and deal bonus damage equal to 2× the combo count on your next hit. A Combo 5 reset = +10 bonus damage on the next swing. Spend the combo for a finisher, then rebuild.

Move through every enemy within Close range, making one attack against each. You cannot be attacked during the movement. Resolve all attacks, then end in any position within Close range. Once per encounter. The signature Dancer move — a single graceful pass through the entire battlefield.
While you moved 5+ tiles this turn: enemies cannot make opportunity attacks against you for any reason. You are too fast to punish. Complete freedom of movement through enemy lines.

Your light weapons deal +1 damage die (Precision Blade goes from 1d6 to 2d6, Combat Knife from 1d4 to 2d4). The Dancer's individual hit damage finally scales. At T4, you've earned bigger numbers per swing on top of your hit volume.
